questions.question: Human factors psychologists primarily aim to:

options.text: Increase employee job satisfaction through better pay
Design systems and tools that fit human capabilities and reduce errors
Select the best candidates for job positions

questions.explanation: Human factors psychology focuses on designing systems, tools, and work environments that accommodate human cognitive and physical capabilities, thereby reducing errors and improving safety and efficiency.

questions.question: When a worker makes a mistake because a control panel displays too much information at once, this is best explained by the concept of:
questions.explanation: Cognitive load refers to the total amount of mental effort required to complete a task. When cognitive demands exceed a worker's capacity — such as when too much information is displayed at once — errors increase. Good design manages cognitive load to reduce errors.
